rationale_for_baseline_tier: An uncomplicated common cold at day 3 with a negative respiratory, sinusitis and meningism screen is self-limiting and managed with symptomatic self-care (T0). The subjective 'can't breathe' is nasal obstruction, not chest pathology. Respiratory and meningism safety-nets to T4/T5 are mandatory as the dangerous mimics.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Genuine shortness of breath at rest, wheeze, chest pain, coughing up discoloured or bloody phlegm, or bluish lips
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Same day — attend ED (000 if severe)
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you become truly short of breath at rest, start wheezing, get chest pain, or cough up green/brown/bloody phlegm — go to the Emergency Department the same day (call 000 if severe).

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Stiff neck, dislike of light, severe headache, or a rash that doesn't fade under pressure
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you develop a stiff neck, can't tolerate light, get a severe headache, or a rash that doesn't fade when pressed — call 000 immediately.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Symptoms beyond 10 days, or severe one-sided facial pain / thick discoloured discharge / double-sickening
escalation_edges.rationale: Suggests bacterial sinusitis warranting GP assessment; not urgent.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Provide clear reassurance and explicit red flags; anxiety about breathing is addressed by explaining nasal vs chest breathing.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is a common cold you can manage at home: saline spray, paracetamol, sleep propped up with a humidifier, and the medicated decongestant spray for no more than 3 days. It should ease over the next week.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Self-care; GP review if not improving by 10 days; ED/000 for breathing difficulty or meningism features
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000 for any true breathing difficulty or stiff neck with severe headache; otherwise self-care overnight.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Uncomplicated acute viral rhinopharyngitis (day 3); respiratory, sinusitis and meningism screens negative; symptomatic care with <=3-day decongestant limit.

critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An evolving pneumonia or early meningitis is dismissed as 'just a cold' and presents late.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Not screening for rest dyspnoea/chest signs before reassurance given the breathing-distress framing.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Sending an uncomplicated cold with a negative respiratory screen to ED — unnecessary emergency resource use.
